title: fix: model monitor health check excludes user errors
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Filtering the noise
gist.blurb: Updated our health check logic. We stopped counting user errors (like bad API keys) as 'successful' traffic, so now we can see when models are actually struggling.
gist.summary: Modified the health check denominator to exclude 4xx user errors, ensuring 5xx server errors aren't diluted by authentication or balance failures.
gist.impact: Model status reporting is now significantly more accurate, preventing broken models from appearing healthy during high traffic.
gist.visual_concept: The Monitor Robot performing a precise sorting task, separating 'user error' items from 'system health' items to get a clear view. The Bee assists with quality control.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art of the robot mascot carefully sorting colored data blocks on a desk, separating grey blocks from red ones. The bee mascot watches with a magnifying glass. A monitor in the background shows a clean lime green (#ecf874) graph. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic. Cozy pixel art, chunky sprites, Stardew Valley vibes.
